question: A team is choosing between using a `business rule task`, a `script task`, and an `undefined task` in a Camunda 8.8 process. According to the Tasks section of the BPMN coverage page, which statement is correct?

options.text: Only the business rule task is supported; script tasks and undefined tasks are display-only symbols.
All three task types — business rule task, script task, and undefined task — are listed as implemented in Camunda 8.8.
Only the script task is supported in Camunda 8.8; business rule task and undefined task were removed from coverage.
None of these task types is supported; they are documented but not executable by Zeebe.

a.text: Script tasks and undefined tasks are both shown with the `implemented` marker in the Tasks section, alongside the business rule task.

b.text: The Tasks section of the BPMN coverage page shows service, user, receive, send, business rule, script, manual, and undefined tasks all as implemented in Camunda 8.8. The only task variant in the section that is not marked as implemented is the receive-task-instantiated variant.

c.text: Business rule tasks and undefined tasks remain part of the supported task set; they are not removed from coverage.

d.text: All three task types are explicitly marked as implemented in the BPMN coverage page, so they can be executed.

explanation: The Tasks section of the BPMN coverage page lists each task symbol with an `implemented` CSS class when the engine supports it. Service, user, receive, send, business rule, script, manual, and undefined tasks are all marked as implemented in Camunda 8.8. The receive-task-instantiated variant is the only task in the section shown without the implemented marker.
